# CANDIDATE: VIX_CONDITIONAL_QUALITY_FILTER

**Filed:** 2026-06-18  
**Filer:** chef-nemotron (free-tier autonomous R&D)  
**Type:** filter_change  
**Status:** DRAFT (NEEDS-RATIFICATION per Rule 9)

## Hypothesis

The quality signal (bearish_streak>=3 OR vol_ratio_1.0-1.5) is predictive in high volatility regimes (VIX>20) but noise in low volatility (VIX<=20). By applying this quality filter only when VIX>20, we isolate the regime where the signal has edge, avoiding false signals in low-vol environments that degraded walk-forward performance in prior tests.

## Mechanism

Entry: For the base BEARISH_REJECTION_RIDE_THE_RIBBON strategy, only take trades when BOTH conditions are met:
1. VIX > 20 (using the same VIX feed as existing strategies)
2. (bearish_streak >= 3 OR vol_ratio between 1.0 and 1.5 inclusive)
Exit: Unchanged from base strategy (premium stop at -8%, TP1 at +30%, ribbon trail management, time stop 15:50 ET).  
State: No additional state tracking beyond existing VIX and quality metrics.

## OP-20 disclosures

1. **Account-size assumption:** Base strategy uses 3 contracts minimum at ~$1.00 entry premium. $1K account can trade 3 contracts (30% of account risk cap). Headline P&L scales linearly with qty; $1K paper realizes ~14% of headline P&L for qty=28 (per OP-20 disclosure example).
2. **Sample bias:** VIX>20 regime sample size unknown without Stage-1 backtest. Selection method: apply quality filter only to bars where VIX>20 in 16-month SPY 5m dataset. Overfit risk: regime conditioning reduces effective sample size; may overfit if VIX>20 periods are not persistent or edge is driven by few extreme days.
3. **Out-of-sample:** NEEDS-OOS (walk-forward held-out window not yet performed)
4. **Real-fills:** NEEDS-REAL-FILLS (top 3 J days not yet checked with realistic OPRA simulation)
5. **Failure modes:** 
   - Worst day: Strategy takes losing trade in VIX>20 regime on J loser day (e.g., 5/05), increasing loss beyond baseline.
   - Max drawdown: Could exceed baseline if filter causes missed winners and taken losers in high-vol regime.
   - Blow-up scenario: Quality signal fails persistently in VIX>20 regime (e.g., during volatile chop), triggering multiple consecutive losses.
6. **Concentration:** unknown -- requires Stage-1 backtest to determine if top-5 days drive P&L (risk if edge relies on few high-vol days).

## Pre-merge gate

Gym validators (7/7 PASS), walk-forward OOS/IS ratio >=0.5, real-fills on top 3 J days (NEEDS-REAL-FILLS until submission).

## Confidence

5 / 10 -- Hypothesis is plausible (regime-dependent signal) but untested; prior quality filter failed due to regime-dependence, so isolating VIX>20 may work. Confidence limited by unknown VIX distribution on anchor days and risk of overfit to regime.

## Pre-existing leaderboard impact

Does not conflict with existing candidates in _LEADERBOARD.md as it is a filter change on base BEARISH_REJECTION_RIDE_THE_RIBBON strategy. May complement watcher proposals (e.g., V14E_BEAR_ONLY_GATE) by adding regime-conditioned entry filter. No overlap with new trade classes (e.g., SNIPER, TBR) as it modifies existing PUT strategy logic.
